refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into monitoring, control, and optimization clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved and verified.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and confirming it meets ≤ E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy, control actions, and optimization effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control operational parameters to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system behavior based on monitored data to continuously improve energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and load sensors integrated with telemetry for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive control algorithms adjusting vehicle operation parameters.
DP3: Use data analytics and machine learning modules to optimize energy efficiency over time.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: DP2 depends on DP1 data input.
DP3 depends on outputs from DP1 and DP2.
coupling_check.notes: The coupling is logical and expected; modular separation supports manageable interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Risk of control algorithm conflicts with safety policies.
Risk that optimization may increase complexity and reduce transparency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure safety constraints are integrated into control and optimization DPs explicitly.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system including vehicle sensors, control logic, and optimization analytics.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time data exchange between Vehicle sensors and NGHE Solution analytics and control modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Control commands sent from NGHE Solution to Vehicle for operational parameter adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety alerts received from Safety Services to constrain control and optimization actions.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone supporting data and command exchange among modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes energy efficiency logic and data processing; low diffusion risk.
Hosts sensors and executes control commands; moderate diffusion due to embedded control.
Limited role in energy efficiency; mostly operational control by human operators.
Provides safety alerts influencing control but does not implement energy efficiency logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency functionality is best contained within the NGHE Solution module to centralize logic and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in NGHE Solution module due to integration of monitoring, control, and optimization.
Dependency on Vehicle sensors and Safety Services interfaces may introduce coupling risks.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider modular sub-partitioning within NGHE Solution to separate monitoring, control, and optimization for maintainability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched by selected DPs with clear traceability and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists in Vehicle due to embedded control execution.
NGHE Solution centralization reduces diffusion risk overall.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as primary owner; interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and energy efficiency metrics at NGHE Solution level.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution with interfaces to Vehicle and Safety Services.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document safety constraints integration in control and optimization DPs.
Plan for modular decomposition within NGHE Solution to manage complexity.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy requirements with clear traceability, manageable coupling, and containment. Safety criticality is addressed by including SafetyAlert interface and recommending explicit safety constraints.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control operational parameters to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system behavior based on monitored data to continuously improve energy efficiency.

revise.why: To explicitly incorporate safety constraints into control and optimization design parameters.

assumptions: Reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Safety alerts effectively constrain control actions.
open_risks: Control algorithm conflicts with safety policies.
evidence_needed: Verification of control algorithm compliance with safety constraints.
Demonstration of energy efficiency improvements meeting E_target.
